title: Streams, Super Streams, and Workload Choice · Go Build Room
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Queue Stream Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 6148 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Queue Stream Semantics.

For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Queue Stream Semantics, keep Queue Stream Semantics connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Workload Selection Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop Go messaging team handles case 2719 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch. In Go, amqp091-go queue consumption automatically exposes every stream protocol capability.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 1100 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence, keep Workload Selection Evidence connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Workload Selection Evidence symptom.

content.value: RabbitMQ Stream Go Client — Controls the client boundary between AMQP 0-9-1 queue and stream use and the native RabbitMQ stream protocol for retention, replay, super streams, and high throughput.

stimulus.title: Workload Selection Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch. In Go, amqp091-go queue consumption automatically exposes every stream protocol capability.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Workload Selection Evidence happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Workload Selection Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Workload Selection Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 3433 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; reject stale connection and channel generations; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Workload Selection Evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
